messages.content: springboot application.properties file properties list with small description,for various case uses,segregated and sorted

1. Server properties

   - server.port: Specifies the port on which the application runs.
   - server.address: Defines the address on which the application listens.
   - server.servlet.context-path: Sets the context path for the application.

2. Datasource properties

   - spring.datasource.url: Specifies the URL for the database connection.
   - spring.datasource.username: Sets the database username.
   - spring.datasource.password: Sets the database password.
   - spring.datasource.driver-class-name: Defines the database driver class name.
   - spring.datasource.hikari.max-lifetime: Sets the maximum lifetime for the Hikari connection pool.

3. JPA (Java Persistence API) properties

   - spring.jpa.show-sql: Enables the display of executed SQL queries on the console.
   - spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: Configures the Hibernate schema generation mode.
   - spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql: Formats the SQL queries to make them more readable.

4. Logging properties

   - logging.level.org.springframework: Sets the log level for Spring framework packages.
   - logging.level.org.hibernate: Defines the log level for Hibernate packages.
   - logging.file.name: Specifies the name of the log file.
   - logging.file.path: Sets the path where the log file is saved.

5. Thymeleaf properties

   - spring.thymeleaf.prefix: Specifies the location of Thymeleaf templates.
   - spring.thymeleaf.suffix: Indicates the extension of Thymeleaf template files.
   - spring.thymeleaf.cache: Enables/disables the Thymeleaf template cache.

6. Cache properties

   - spring.cache.type: Defines the cache type to use (e.g., simple, caffeine).
   - spring.cache.caffeine.spec: Sets the specification for the Caffeine cache.

7. Security properties

   - spring.security.user.name: Specifies the default username for the application.
   - spring.security.user.password: Defines the default password for the application.
   - spring.security.oauth2.client.registration: Configures OAuth2 client registrations.

8. Actuator properties

   - management.endpoints.web.exposure.include: Specifies the endpoints to expose by the actuator.
   - management.endpoint.health.show-details: Configures the health details visibility.
   - management.endpoint.metrics.enabled: Enables/disables the metrics endpoint.

9. Misc properties

   - spring.profiles.active: Determines the active profile for the application.
   - spring.application.name: Sets the application name.
   - spring.main.banner-mode: Configures the display of the Spring Boot banner.
continue more

10. Mail properties

   - spring.mail.host: Specifies the mail server host.
   - spring.mail.port: Sets the mail server port.
   - spring.mail.username: Defines the username for the mail server.
   - spring.mail.password: Configures the password for the mail server.
   - spring.mail.properties.mail.smtp.auth: Enables SMTP authentication.
   - spring.mail.properties.mail.smtp.starttls.enable: Enables STARTTLS encryption for SMTP.

11. File storage properties

   - spring.resources.static-locations: Configures the locations for serving static files.
   - file.upload-dir: Specifies the directory where uploaded files are stored.

12. Internationalization (i18n) properties

   - spring.messages.basename: Specifies the base file name for message resources.
   - spring.messages.cache-duration: Sets the cache duration for message resources.
   - spring.messages.encoding: Configures the character encoding used for message resources.

13. Web properties

   - spring.mvc.view.prefix: Specifies the location of the view templates.
   - spring.mvc.view.suffix: Indicates the extension of view template files.

14. Batch properties

   - spring.batch.job.names: Configures which jobs are executed by the application.
   - spring.batch.initialize-schema: Specifies when the batch schema is initialized.
   - spring.batch.job.enabled: Enables/disables the execution of batch jobs at startup.

15. WebSocket properties

   - spring.websocket.enabled: Enables or disables WebSockets in the application.
   - spring.websocket.sockjs.enabled: Enables or disables SockJS fallback options.

16. Session properties

   - server.servlet.session.timeout: Sets the session timeout for the application.
   - server.servlet.session.cookie.name: Specifies the name of the session cookie.
   - server.servlet.session.cookie.http-only: Configures the HttpOnly flag for the session cookie.

17. Embedded server properties

   - server.tomcat.accesslog.enabled: Enables/disables the Tomcat access log.
   - server.tomcat.accesslog.pattern: Specifies the pattern for the Tomcat access log.
   - server.tomcat.max-threads: Sets the maximum number of threads for the Tomcat server.
   - server.undertow.buffer-cache-size: Configures the buffer cache size for the Undertow server.

18. SSL properties

   - server.ssl.enabled: Enables/disables SSL for the application.
   - server.ssl.key-store: Specifies the location of the key store.
   - server.ssl.key-store-password: Sets the password for the key store.
   - server.ssl.key-alias: Defines the alias of the key in the key store.

19. Health check properties

   - spring.boot.admin.client.enabled: Enables/disables registration with the Spring Boot Admin server.
   - spring.boot.admin.client.url: Specifies the URL of the Spring Boot Admin server.

20. RabbitMQ properties

   - spring.rabbitmq.host: Specifies the RabbitMQ server host.
   - spring.rabbitmq.port: Sets the RabbitMQ server port.
   - spring.rabbitmq.username: Defines the username for the RabbitMQ server.
   - spring.rabbitmq.password: Configures the password for the RabbitMQ server.
continue more

21. Kafka properties

   - spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers: Specifies the Kafka server addresses.
   - spring.kafka.consumer.group-id: Defines the Kafka consumer group ID.
   - spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset: Configures the Kafka consumer auto offset reset strategy.
   - spring.kafka.producer.key-serializer: Sets the Kafka producer key serializer.
   - spring.kafka.producer.value-serializer: Sets the Kafka producer value serializer.

22. Redis properties

   - spring.redis.host: Specifies the Redis server host.
   - spring.redis.password: Defines the Redis server password.
   - spring.redis.port: Sets the Redis server port.
   - spring.redis.ssl.enabled: Configures SSL for Redis connections.
   - spring.redis.timeout: Sets the connection timeout for Redis.

23. MongoDB properties

   - spring.data.mongodb.uri: Specifies the MongoDB connection URI.
   - spring.data.mongodb.database: Defines the MongoDB database name.
   - spring.data.mongodb.authentication-database: Specifies the MongoDB authentication database.
   - spring.data.mongodb.username: Sets the MongoDB username.
   - spring.data.mongodb.password: Configures the MongoDB password.

24. Elasticsearch properties

   - spring.data.elasticsearch.cluster-name: Specifies the Elasticsearch cluster name.
   - spring.data.elasticsearch.cluster-nodes: Sets the Elasticsearch cluster nodes.
   - spring.data.elasticsearch.repositories.enabled: Configures the Elasticsearch repositories.
   - spring.data.elasticsearch.rest.uris: Defines the Elasticsearch REST client URIs.

25. CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) properties

   - spring.webflux.cors.allowed-origins: Specifies allowed origins for CORS requests.
   - spring.webflux.cors.allowed-methods: Configures the allowed methods for CORS requests.
   - spring.webflux.cors.allowed-headers: Defines the allowed headers for CORS requests.

26. Freemarker properties

   - spring.freemarker.prefix: Specifies the location of Freemarker templates.
   - spring.freemarker.suffix: Indicates the extension of Freemarker template files.
   - spring.freemarker.cache: Enables/disables the Freemarker template cache.

27. Velocity properties

   - spring.velocity.prefix: Specifies the location of Velocity templates.
   - spring.velocity.suffix: Indicates the extension of Velocity template files.
   - spring.velocity.cache: Enables/disables the Velocity template cache.

28. JWT (JSON Web Token) properties

   - jwt.secret: Defines the JWT secret key.
   - jwt.expiration: Sets JWT token expiration time.

29. Hazelcast properties

   - spring.hazelcast.config: Specifies the path to the Hazelcast configuration file.

30. RestTemplate properties

   - spring.resttemplate.connection-timeout: Sets the connection timeout for RestTemplate.
   - spring.resttemplate.read-timeout: Configures the read timeout for RestTemplate.

These properties cover various use cases for a Spring Boot application. However, note that not all properties may be applicable for every application. Depending on the chosen features, integrations, and libraries, an application will require a different combination of properties to function optimally.
continue more

31. Flyway properties

   - spring.flyway.enabled: Enables/disables Flyway migrations.
   - spring.flyway.locations: Specifies the locations for Flyway migration scripts.
   - spring.flyway.baseline-on-migrate: Configures whether to create a baseline on migrations.
   - spring.flyway.placeholder-replacement: Defines whether Flyway replaces placeholders.

32. Liquibase properties

   - spring.liquibase.enabled: Enables/disables Liquibase migrations.
   - spring.liquibase.change-log: Specifies the location of the Liquibase change log file.
   - spring.liquibase.contexts: Configures the contexts for the Liquibase migrations.
   - spring.liquibase.default-schema: Sets the default schema for Liquibase migrations.

33. Apache Camel properties

   - camel.springboot.name: Specifies the Apache Camel application name.
   - camel.springboot.xml-routes: Sets the location of the XML route definitions.
   - camel.component.servlet.mapping: Configures the servlet mapping for the Camel servlet.

34. Apache Kafka Stream properties

   - spring.kafka.streams.application-id: Specifies the application ID for Kafka Streams.
   - spring.kafka.streams.properties: Configures Kafka Streams properties.
   - spring.kafka.streams.default-key-serde: Sets the default key SerDe for Kafka Streams.
   - spring.kafka.streams.default-value-serde: Sets the default value SerDe for Kafka Streams.

35. Solr properties

   - spring.data.solr.host: Specifies the Solr server URL.
   - spring.data.solr.repositories.enabled: Configures Solr repositories.

36. JCache (JSR-107) properties

   - spring.cache.jcache.config: Specifies the location of the JSR-107 configuration file.
   - spring.cache.jcache.provider: Configures the JSR-107 provider.

37. GraphQL properties

   - graphql.servlet.mapping: Specifies the endpoint path for the GraphQL mapping.
   - graphql.servlet.enabled: Enables/disables the GraphQL servlet.
   - graphql.servlet.cors-enabled: Enables/disables CORS for the GraphQL servlet.

38. Task scheduling properties

   - spring.task.scheduling.pool.size: Sets the thread pool size for the task scheduler.
   - spring.task.execution.pool.size: Configures the thread pool size for the task executor.

39. WebSocket STOMP properties

   - spring.websocket.stomp.buffer-size: Specifies the buffer size for WebSocket STOMP.
   - spring.websocket.stomp.send-buffer-size-limit: Sets the buffer size limit for WebSocket STOMP.

40. Spring Integration properties

   - spring.integration.poller.fixed-delay: Defines the polling delay for Spring Integration.
   - spring.integration.error-handler: Configures the error handler for Spring Integration.
   - spring.integration.channel.auto-create: Enables/disables channel auto-creation in Spring Integration.

These properties cover even more technical use cases for Spring Boot applications. While not all properties may be applicable to every application, they can be a helpful reference when configuring specific features or third-party libraries. Keep in mind that the appropriate settings will depend on the desired functionality for a given application.
continue more

41. Swagger properties

   - springfox.documentation.swagger.v2.path: Specifies the path for serving the Swagger UI.
   - springfox.documentation.enabled: Enables/disables Swagger documentation.
   - springfox.documentation.auto-configuration.strategy: Sets the auto-configuration strategy for Swagger documentation.

42. RSocket properties

   - spring.rsocket.server.transport: Configures the transport for RSocket server.
   - spring.rsocket.server.port: Sets the port for RSocket server.
   - spring.rsocket.client.port: Sets the port for RSocket client connections.

43. gRPC properties

   - spring.grpc.server.port: Specifies the gRPC server port.
   - spring.grpc.client.address: Defines the gRPC client server address.
   - spring.grpc.client.negotiation-type: Sets the gRPC client negotiation type.

44. JMX (Java Management Extensions) properties

   - spring.jmx.enabled: Enables/disables JMX support for the application.
   - spring.jmx.mbean-domain: Specifies the JMX MBean domain name.
   - spring.jmx.server: Configures the JMX server instance.

45. XML properties

   - spring.xml.ignore: Specifies whether to ignore XML files at startup.
   - spring.xml.entity-resolution: Configures the entity resolution strategy for XML files.

46. Amazon Web Services (AWS) properties

   - cloud.aws.stack-name: Specifies the AWS stack name for the application.
   - cloud.aws.region.auto: Configures AWS region auto-detection.
   - cloud.aws.region.static: Sets the static AWS region for the application.

47. Google Cloud properties

   - spring.cloud.gcp.project-id: Specifies the Google Cloud project ID.
   - spring.cloud.gcp.credentials.location: Sets the location for the Google Cloud credentials.

48. Vault properties

   - spring.cloud.vault.authentication: Specifies the authentication method for Vault.
   - spring.cloud.vault.uri: Defines the Vault server URI.
   - spring.cloud.vault.token: Sets the token for Vault authentication.

49. Rate limiting properties

   - spring.cloud.gateway.filter.request-rate-limiter.redis-enabled: Enables/disables Redis for request rate limiting.
   - spring.zuul.ratelimit.repository.redis.enabled: Enables/disables Redis for rate-limiting in Zuul.

50. Consul properties

   - spring.cloud.consul.host: Specifies the Consul server host.
   - spring.cloud.consul.port: Sets the Consul server port.
   - spring.cloud.consul.discovery.health-check-path: Configures the health check path for Consul.

These properties provide additional options for configuring Spring Boot applications that utilize various technologies, such as Swagger API documentation, messaging protocols like RSocket and gRPC, JMX support, cloud services, and rate limiting. When implementing these features or integrating with these services, be sure to adjust the properties accordingly in the `application.properties` file.
continue more

51. Eureka properties

   - spring.cloud.netflix.eureka.client.service-url.default-zone: Specifies the Eureka server URL.
   - eureka.instance.health-check-url-path: Configures the health check URL path for Eureka.
   - eureka.instance.hostname: Sets the instance hostname for Eureka registration.

52. Reactive properties

   - spring.webflux.base-path: Specifies the base path for WebFlux applications.
   - spring.webflux.exclude: Configures the packages to exclude from WebFlux autoconfiguration.

53. Job scheduling properties

   - spring.quartz.job-store-type: Specifies the job store type for Quartz.
   - spring.quartz.properties.org.quartz.scheduler.instanceName: Sets the Quartz scheduler instance name.

54. Zipkin properties

   - spring.zipkin.enabled: Enables/disables the Zipkin distributed tracing.
   - spring.zipkin.baseUrl: Specifies the Zipkin server base URL.
   - spring.zipkin.sender.type: Configures the sender for sending tracing data to Zipkin.

55. Turbine properties

   - spring.cloud.turbine.enabled: Enables/disables Turbine for Hystrix stream aggregation.
   - spring.cloud.turbine.app-config: Configures the application names for Turbine stream aggregation.

56. External system properties

   - spring.autoconfigure.exclude: Specifies auto-configurations to exclude from the project.
   - spring.profiles.group: Configures groups of profiles to be activated together.

57. Logstash properties

   - spring.logstash.url: Specifies the Logstash server URL.
   - spring.logstash.prefix: Configures the prefix for sending logs to Logstash.

58. Graphite properties

   - management.metrics.export.graphite.enabled: Enables/disables exporting metrics to Graphite.
   - management.metrics.export.graphite.host: Specifies the Graphite server host.
   - management.metrics.export.graphite.port: Sets the Graphite server port.

59. New Relic properties

   - management.metrics.export.new-relic.enabled: Enables/disables exporting metrics to New Relic.
   - management.metrics.export.new-relic.api-key: Defines the API key for New Relic.

60. Jaeger properties

   - spring.jaeger.enabled: Enables/disables Jaeger support for tracing.
   - spring.jaeger.service-name: Specifies the Jaeger service name.
   - spring.jaeger.protocol: Configures the protocol to use for Jaeger communication.

These additional properties cover more specific use cases for Spring Boot applications, such as various discovery services like Eureka and Consul, distributed tracing options like Zipkin and Jaeger, job scheduling with Quartz, and sending metrics or logs to different monitoring platforms like Graphite, New Relic, and Logstash. Depending on the features and integrations your application requires, you may need to include one or more of these properties in your `application.properties` file.
continue more

61. InfluxDB properties

   - spring.influx.url: Specifies the InfluxDB server URL.
   - spring.influx.user: Configures the InfluxDB user.
   - spring.influx.password: Sets the InfluxDB password.
   - spring.influx.database: Defines the InfluxDB database to use.

62. Template engines properties

   - spring.mustache.prefix: Specifies the location of Mustache templates.
   - spring.mustache.suffix: Indicates the extension of Mustache template files.
   - spring.mustache.cache: Enables/disables the Mustache template cache.
   - spring.groovy.template.configuration.cache: Enables/disables the Groovy template cache.

63. Route caching properties

   - spring.cloud.gateway.route-locator.cache: Specifies the cache time-to-live duration for Gateway route locator.
   - spring.cloud.zuul.ratelimit.prefix: Configures the cache prefix for rate limiting in Zuul.

64. Apache Camel cluster properties

   - camel.springcloud.cluster.service-call.service-filter: Sets the service-filter used for clustering in Apache Camel Spring Cloud.

65. SQL Server properties

   - spring.datasource.sqlserver.port: Specifies the SQL Server database port.
   - spring.datasource.sqlserver.instance: Sets the SQL Server named instance.

66. Okta properties

   - okta.oauth2.client-id: Specifies the Okta OAuth2 client ID.
   - okta.oauth2.client-secret: Sets the Okta OAuth2 client secret.
   - okta.oauth2.issuer: Configures the Okta issuer URL.

67. Prometheus properties

   - management.metrics.export.prometheus.enabled: Enables/disables exporting metrics to Prometheus.
   - management.endpoint.prometheus.enabled: Enables/disables Prometheus metrics endpoint.

68. Jetty properties

   - server.jetty.accesslog.append: Determines whether access logs should be appended to existing files.
   - server.jetty.accesslog.date-format: Configures the date format in access logs.

69. Apache Cassandra properties

   - spring.data.cassandra.contact-points: Specifies the Cassandra node contact points.
   - spring.data.cassandra.keyspace-name: Configures the keyspace name for Cassandra.

70. Docker properties

   - spring.boot.docker.layers.enabled: Enables/disables Docker layering for the application.

These properties offer finer-grained configuration for Spring Boot applications that interact with additional technologies, databases, templating engines, cloud platforms, and other integrations. While these properties may not be relevant for every Spring Boot application, they can be useful when developing applications that rely on specific libraries or services. Tailor your `application.properties` file to your application's unique requirements by incorporating and modifying these properties as necessary.
continue more

71. Kryo properties

   - spring.session.hazelcast.map-name: Specifies the Kryo serialization name for Hazelcast.
   - spring.session.hazelcast.serializer: Sets the Kryo serializer for Hazelcast.

72. OpenAPI properties

   - springdoc.api-docs.title: Specifies the title for the OpenAPI API documentation.
   - springdoc.api-docs.description: Configures the description for the OpenAPI API documentation.
   - springdoc.api-docs.version: Sets the version of the OpenAPI API documentation.

73. Oauth2 Resource Server properties

   - spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri: Configures the issuer URI for OAuth2 resource server.
   - spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.jwk-set-uri: Specifies the JWK Set URI for OAuth2 resource server.

74. Neo4j properties

   - spring.data.neo4j.uri: Specifies the connection URI for Neo4j.
   - spring.data.neo4j.username: Sets the Neo4j username.
   - spring.data.neo4j.password: Configures the Neo4j password.

75. Metrics properties

   - management.metrics.distribution.percentiles-histogram: Configures histogram bucket tracking for Spring Boot metrics.
   - management.metrics.enable: Enables or disables the collection of metrics.

76. Cache provider properties

   - spring.cache.ehcache.config: Specifies the configuration file for Ehcache.
   - spring.cache.infinispan.config: Configures the Infinispan configuration file.
   - spring.cache.couchbase.bucket: Sets the Couchbase bucket for caching.

77. JsonPath Cache properties

   - spring.json-path-cache.cache-limits.expression: Configures the caching limits in JsonPath Cache.

78. Hystrix properties

   - hystrix.command.default.execution.isolation.strategy: Sets the isolation strategy for Hystrix commands.

79. Header propagation properties

   - spring.cloud.stream.default.producer.headerMode: Configures the header mode for Spring Cloud Stream producers.

80. Ignite properties

   - spring.cache.ignite.cfg: Specifies the path to the Ignite configuration file.

These configuration properties extend the flexibility and customization options for Spring Boot applications leveraging various areas like caching providers, API documentation, security, databases, and more. It's important to remember that not all of these properties will be applicable in every scenario, and their inclusion should correspond to the specific demands of a given project's features and requirements. Update the `application.properties` file with the necessary properties to optimize and support the unique aspects of each Spring Boot application.
continue more

81. Data Rest properties

   - spring.data.rest.base-path: Specifies the base path for Data REST endpoints.
   - spring.data.rest.output-path: Configures the output path for Data REST.
   - spring.data.rest.default-page-size: Determines the default page size for Data REST pagination.

82. Oracle properties

   - spring.datasource.oracle.url: Specifies the Oracle database connection URL.
   - spring.datasource.oracle.username: Sets the Oracle database username.
   - spring.datasource.oracle.password: Configures the Oracle database password.

83. Vert.x properties

   - vertx.options.eventLoopPoolSize: Specifies the event loop pool size for Vert.x.
   - vertx.options.workerPoolSize: Sets the worker pool size for Vert.x.
   - vertx.options.blockedThreadCheckInterval: Configures the blocked thread check interval for Vert.x.

84. SAP properties

   - spring.sap.jco.ashost: Specifies the SAP application host.
   - spring.sap.jco.client: Configures the SAP client number.
   - spring.sap.jco.user: Sets the SAP username.
   - spring.sap.jco.passwd: Sets the SAP password.

85. Google Cloud Storage properties

   - spring.cloud.gcp.storage.auto-create-files: Enables or disables auto-creation of files in Google Cloud Storage.
   - spring.cloud.gcp.storage.bucket: Specifies the Google Cloud Storage bucket.
 
86. OAuth2 client properties

   - spring.security.oauth2.client.registration.client-id: Configures the client ID of the OAuth2 client.
   - spring.security.oauth2.client.registration.client-secret: Sets the client secret of the OAuth2 client.

87. Micrometer Atlas properties

   - management.metrics.export.atlas.enabled: Enables or disables exporting metrics to Micrometer Atlas Registry.
   - management.metrics.export.atlas.step: Configures the publishing interval of metrics for Micrometer Atlas Registry.

88. Micrometer Datadog properties

   - management.metrics.export.datadog.enabled: Enables or disables exporting metrics to Micrometer Datadog Registry.
   - management.metrics.export.datadog.api-key: Sets the API key for Micrometer Datadog Registry.

89. Micrometer Wavefront properties

   - management.metrics.export.wavefront.enabled: Enables or disables exporting metrics to Micrometer Wavefront Registry.
   - management.metrics.export.wavefront.uri: Specifies the URI for the Micrometer Wavefront Registry.

90. Micrometer Stackdriver properties

   - management.metrics.export.stackdriver.enabled: Enables or disables exporting metrics to Micrometer Stackdriver Registry.
   - management.metrics.export.stackdriver.resource-type: Configures the resource type for Micrometer Stackdriver Registry.

As the list of properties continues, the focus is on more niche areas in Spring Boot applications, such as integration with specific databases (e.g., Oracle), cloud storage services, specific monitoring platforms, or specific messaging frameworks. As always, it's essential to only include those property configurations relevant to your application's specific requirements and implemented technologies. Updating the `application.properties` file will continue to enhance the project's efficiency and effectiveness as more specialized properties are added.
continue more

91. H2 server properties

   - spring.h2.console.enabled: Enables/disables the H2 console.
   - spring.h2.console.path: Specifies the path of the H2 console.
   - spring.h2.console.settings.web-allow-others: Configures access permissions for the H2 console.

92. OAuth2 Authorization Server properties

   - spring.security.oauth2.authorization.check-token-access: Configures token access rules for OAuth2 authorization server.
   - spring.security.oauth2.authorization.token-key-access: Sets token key access for OAuth2 authorization server.

93. GraphQL DataLoader properties

   - graphql.servlet.data-loader-registry: Specifies the data loader registry for GraphQL servlet.

94. Apache Ignite properties

   - spring.cache.ignite.instance-name: Configures the instance name for Apache Ignite.
   - spring.cache.ignite.grid-name: Specifies the grid name for Apache Ignite.

95. Azure Event Hubs properties

   - spring.integration.azure.eventhub.checkpoint-mode: Configures the checkpoint mode for Azure Event Hubs integration.
   - spring.integration.azure.eventhub.consumer-group: Specifies the Azure Event Hubs consumer group.

96. TLS (Transport Layer Security) properties

   - server.ssl.enabled-protocols: Configures the enabled SSL protocols for the server.
   - server.ssl.ciphers: Specifies SSL ciphers to be used by the server.

97. FirebaseFirestore properties

   - spring.cloud.gcp.firestore.project-id: Specifies the Google Cloud Firestore project ID.
   - spring.cloud.gcp.firestore.credentials.location: Configures the location for Google Cloud Firestore credentials.

98. PostgreSQL properties

   - spring.datasource.postgresql.schema: Specifies the PostgreSQL schema to be used.
   - spring.datasource.postgresql.search-path: Sets the search path for PostgreSQL.

99. Amazon S3 properties

   - spring.cloud.aws.s3.bucket: Specifies the Amazon S3 bucket.
   - spring.cloud.aws.s3.region: Configures the Amazon S3 region.

100. Custom namespace properties

   - myapp.custom.property: Configures a custom property for your specific application.

These final properties address additional niche use cases such as working with specific databases, cloud storage and messaging services, GraphQL DataLoader registry, enabling SSL protocols and ciphers, and custom namespaces. Not every Spring Boot application will utilize these properties, but they can be helpful when integrating with specific services or customizing your apps. Configure your project's `application.properties` to cater to any pertinent technologies or requirements you may be working with based on your application's unique demands.
